seoTitle: Possum Creek vs Potato Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Possum Creek and Potato Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Possum Creek vs Potato Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Possum Creek and Potato Point.
heading: Possum Creek vs Potato Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Possum Creek, New South Wales and Potato Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Potato Point has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Possum Creek (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Possum Creek or Potato Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Possum Creek has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Potato Point scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Possum Creek scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Potato Point scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Possum Creek or Potato Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Potato Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
